verdicts.note: Only evidence is the pamphlet's unspecific 'make the tax system fairer' [S1]; +1 with low confidence is exactly the right calibration and low is correctly excluded from alignment math.
Trust Fund expansion, funded right-to-counsel, more counselors/mental-health staff [S2] are consistently invest-and-expand; no audit/restraint planks anywhere in the dossier.

Borderline on confidence: all evidence is one source (S2), but it contains three distinct public-tools planks (Housing Trust Fund, tenant right-to-counsel, community land trusts), which reads as repeated explicit statements. Direction +2 is unambiguous — zero market-supply content.

Direction +2 is right — S2 explicitly rejects increased sentencing in favor of root causes, the pole_b flagship. But this is a single platform statement from one source, not a record or repeated statements; the guide caps that at medium. Evans's equivalent single-statement safety score was correctly given medium — this should match.
Newcomer status corroborated by news [S4] and the pamphlet's anti-'career politicians' framing [S1]; two sources support high.
Platform plank on immigrant protections and language access [S2] plus KIND immigration case support [S1]; +1 medium is proportionate — no ICE-constraint or sanctuary specifics that would justify +2.
S2 is entirely within-system (out-of-pocket costs, insurer/hospital pricing accountability, community mental health); no single-payer language anywhere. -1 medium fits.
Single S2 rights plank (reproductive freedom, civil rights, due process) supports +1 medium; scorer correctly resisted +2 absent gender-affirming-care or curricula specifics.

Checked whether -2 leans on the Libertarian label: it doesn't. Explicit anti-income-tax stance repeated in pamphlet and website [S1][S2], plus blaming 'rising taxes' for housing costs, is direct evidence for fully pole_a.
'Fiscal responsibility, accountable government' repeated across S1 and S2, organized under 'Enact, Reform, and Repeal' with 'transparency, accountability' framing; -1 is if anything conservative.

Supply-side deregulation argued from hands-on developer experience in both sources [S1][S2]; the dossier itself notes 'his housing answer is supply-side deregulation rather than public funding.' Airtight -2.
Well calibrated: energy abundance/affordability plank ties costs to regulation but never names the CCA or carbon pricing, so -1 medium rather than -2 high is exactly right.
Never elected; lost the 2025 Auburn council race [S4]. His stint as State Senate policy staff [S1] gives some insider exposure, but the outsider profile still supports +2.
'Solutions that don't waste your tax dollars' [S1] and results-first framing [S2] are repeated across both sources; high is justified and -1 (not -2) respects that he proposes no audits or cuts.

Checked whether high-rise TOD really maps to pole_a: his only housing answer is dense market supply to lower costs [S1] with no public-tools planks, so a mild -1 medium is defensible; 0 would overread his silence on tenant policy.

Checked for exaggeration given his addiction-recovery volunteering and help-framed language ('into successful treatment') [S1]: mandatory treatment under a three-strikes framework is the rubric's named pole_a marker, and the dossier calls it the field's most coercive stance. -2 with medium (single pamphlet statement) is properly hedged.
First-time legislative candidate who lost a 2025 Kent council primary [S4], running explicitly as an alternative to the incumbent [S1][S2].

Checked direction: HB 2015's local-option tax [S4] is new revenue authority to fund services, not progressive/wealth taxation, so +1 (not +2) is the right magnitude; medium from a single bill is fair even though it is record evidence.
TANF, eviction-prevention, childcare and healthcare-access expansion [S1][S2] are invest-and-expand planks; +1 medium fits since the expansion agenda is platform, not enacted record.

Minor citation nit: the basis mentions her Housing Committee seat, which comes from S1/S3, while only S2 is cited — but the load-bearing evidence (eviction-prevention assistance, safety-net supports) is genuinely in S2, so the score stands.

A documented, genuine blend: $100M funding for criminal-justice agencies (HB 2015, record) [S4] alongside independent deadly-force investigations, anti-bias hiring, and social-worker crisis response [S2]. 0 = 'genuinely mixed' is the textbook use, and high is earned by record plus platform.
Eight years in the seat, two verifiable signed bills [S4][S5], four committee assignments [S3]; the maximal proven-record case. -2 high is airtight.

Consumer data-rights plank (mandatory disclosure, consumer control, informed consent) [S2] maps to the pole_b 'data privacy' marker; +1 medium correctly avoids +2 since she says nothing about AI guardrails or data centers.
